step_by_step_reasoning: We identify nodes on the whiteboard representing the GitOps pipeline: Bitbucket (ThirdParty), API Gateway, Lambda, two S3 buckets, CodeBuild, CodePipeline, and CloudFormation. The flow starts with a commit in Bitbucket, which triggers API Gateway via webhook. API Gateway invokes Lambda, which fetches the code from Bitbucket, zips it, and puts it in the first S3 bucket. This triggers CodeBuild, which calculates checksums to identify only changed files, preventing redundant pipeline executions. CodeBuild uploads these changed files to the second S3 bucket, which triggers CodePipeline to deploy changes via CloudFormation.

graph.name: CleverTap: Automated Infrastructure Deployment Pipeline
graph.notes: Automated GitOps pipeline enabling 100% read-only AWS console access and safe multi-region deployments.

nodes.name: Lambda
nodes.notes: Zips repository source and uploads to S3.

nodes.name: CodeBuild
nodes.notes: Calculates checksums to detect modified files.

nodes.name: CloudFormation
nodes.notes: Applies change sets to manage stacks.
